arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola decision justified after ignoring request following Arsenal clash
arts.sum: MANCHESTER CITY 2-1 WATFORD: Pep Guardiola's side eased to a comfortable Carabao Cup victory over Championship Watford despite Tom Ince's 86th-minute goal making for a close finish

arts.ttl: Arsenal star yet to play this season remains happy amid recent links away
arts.sum: Defender Takehiro Tomiyasu remains happy at Arsenal despite the increased competition in his position and links back to Serie A. The Japanese star didn’t play a minute for Arsenal during pre-season having suffered a knee injury before the Premier League club jetted off for the United States and as a result, the 25-year-old has yet […] The post Arsenal star yet to play this season remains happy amid recent links away appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Report: Arsenal and Tottenham could sign 'one of the best' young defenders in the world for just £25m
arts.sum: Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur are both on the up and will fancy their respective chances of bringing silverware to North London over the coming years....

arts.ttl: Man City wins in League Cup two days after feisty Arsenal match. Nkunku hat trick for Chelsea
arts.sum: Playing just two days after a feisty Premier League match against Arsenal, Manchester City fielded a heavily rotated team and had too much for second-tier Watford in the English League Cup. City won 2-1 thanks to first-half goals by Jeremy Doku and Matheus Nunes at Etihad Stadium as the third round continued Tuesday with Chelsea also advancing by thrashing fourth-division Barrow 5-0, with Christopher Nkunku scoring a hat trick.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola provides Rodri update with Man City left sweating over the severity of the Spanish star's knee injury suffered in 2-2 draw with Arsenal
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has provided an update on the length of Rodri 's expected lay-off after the midfielder sustained a serious knee injury against Arsenal on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta responds to 'dark arts' claims following Man City draw
arts.sum: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta claims that his side played how they "had to" after securing a 2-2 draw against Premier League title rivals Manchester City on Sunday. The Gunners boss took the time to detail who would be available for Wednesday's clash with League One side Bolton in the Carabao Cup, claiming that goalkeeper Raya could miss the fixture after picking up a knee injury against City.

arts.ttl: Roy Keane slams Mikel Arteta for Man City complaints in brutal Arsenal rant
arts.sum: Roy Keane has told Mikel Arteta to 'take his medicine' following Arsenal's clash against Manchester City. Keane, having heard Arteta's remarks, lambasted the Arsenal boss on Sky Sports, insisting he should just accept the referee's call .

arts.ttl: Why Leandro Trossard isn't banned for Arsenal's next Premier League game
arts.sum: Arsenal will have Leandro Trossard available for the next Premier League game at home to Leicester on Saturday afternoon, despite his red card against Manchester City. It is the second time in quick succession that an Arsenal player has received a second yellow card for kicking the ball away after Declan Rice’s controversial dismissal against Brighton & Hove Albion .

arts.ttl: Arsenal face shock competition for Sesko
arts.sum: While Arsenal continue to monitor the Slovenia international’s progress in Germany, they are set to face immense competition for his signature next year. Manchester United are reportedly set to rival Arsenal in the race to sign RB Leipzig forward Benjamin Sesko .

arts.ttl: Arteta could now start one of 'English football's biggest jewels' for Arsenal v Bolton, he's 'unbelievable' - opinion
arts.sum: Arsenal will look to continue on their solid run of form when they get their League Cup campaign under way on Wednesday night. The Gunners...
